fields.Text:
Post-PCN patient with pyonephrosis, fever/rigors, and hypotension. Most likely complication?

{{c1::Transient bacteremia/sepsis}}
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Transient bacteremia and sepsis are the most common complications after percutaneous nephrostomy for pyonephrosis, requiring intensive intra- and post-procedural monitoring and fluid resuscitation. All patients should receive pre-procedural broad-spectrum antibiotics and rapid decompression of the involved renal collecting system with minimal manipulation to reduce risk related to these complications.




Open review Q1

fields.Text: After percutaneous nephrostomy for pyonephrosis, the most common serious complications are {{c1::transient bacteremia and sepsis}}.
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Transient bacteremia and sepsis are the most common complications after percutaneous nephrostomy for pyonephrosis, requiring intensive intra- and post-procedural monitoring and fluid resuscitation. All patients should receive pre-procedural broad-spectrum antibiotics and rapid decompression of the involved renal collecting system with minimal manipulation to reduce risk related to these complications.

fields.Text: CTDIw = {{c1::1/3 central CTDI + 2/3 peripheral CTDI}}, and CTDIvol = {{c2::CTDIw divided by pitch}}.
fields.Extra:
Q2. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: CTDIw (mGy) is closer to the human dose profile compared to CTDI100. CTDIvol (mGy) is obtained by dividing CTDIw by pitch factor.

fields.Text: For the CTDI values center 15 mGy, periphery 30 mGy, pitch 1.25: CTDIw is {{c1::25 mGy}} and CTDIvol is {{c2::20 mGy}}.

fields.Text:
Lymphangiography/embolization case after thoracic surgery. Most likely indication?

{{c1::Chylothorax}}
fields.Extra:
Q3. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Lymphangiography serves both diagnostic and therapeutic purposes in chylothorax. Diagnostically, it identifies leak locations and lymphatic anatomy. Therapeutically, oil contrast agents can occlude lymphatic leak points and provide some symptomatic relief. Coiling is used for more definitive treatment of a clear leak.

fields.Text: Lymphangiography is diagnostic and therapeutic for {{c1::chylothorax}} because oil contrast can identify and sometimes seal lymphatic leaks.

fields.Extra:
Q4. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The most reliable sign of a chronic time frame for deep venous thrombosis (DVT) is establishing its temporal presence by looking at prior imaging.

fields.Text: Ultrasound morphology can suggest chronic DVT, but the most reliable proof of chronicity is {{c1::prior imaging showing temporal presence}}.

What is the most common gynecologic malignancy in the United States?

{{c1::Endometrial cancer}}.

Absent femoral pulses, bilateral claudication, impotence, and aortoiliac occlusion. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Leriche syndrome}}

{{c1::Increase in size}}.

Cochlear promontory lesion. Least likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Congenital cholesteatoma}}

fields.Text:
Infant with intraventricular mass and hydrocephalus. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Choroid plexus papilloma/carcinoma}}
fields.Extra:
Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Differentiation between choroid plexus papilloma and carcinoma is difficult on imaging alone and ultimately depends on histology. Carcinomas typically show extensive parenchymal invasion through the ventricular wall and peritumoral vasogenic edema, while papillomas remain intraventricular. Carcinomas are more heterogeneous due to hemorrhage and cyst formation.





Open review Q10

fields.Text: Choroid plexus carcinoma is favored over papilloma when there is {{c1::ventricular wall invasion, edema, hemorrhage, or cystic heterogeneity}}.
fields.Extra:

Carcinomas are more heterogeneous due to hemorrhage and cyst formation.

fields.Text:
Contrast CT venogram with green arrowed cerebellar vein. Structure?

{{c1::Superior vermian vein}}
fields.Extra:
Q11. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Galenic drainage is the superior venous route where the precentral cerebellar vein and superior vermian vein drain the paleocerebellum to the vein of Galen, with anastomoses to petrosal and tentorial drainage routes providing collateral pathways.

fields.Text: The superior vermian vein drains the paleocerebellum through the {{c1::Galenic venous route}}.

fields.Extra:
Q12. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The standard cardiac MRI imaging planes are derived from the long axis of the heart, and include short axis, horizontal long axis (four-chamber) and vertical long axis (two-chamber) planes.

fields.Text:
Captopril renography study. Radiopharmaceutical?

{{c1::Tc-99m MAG3}}
fields.Extra:
Q13. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Captopril renography is a sensitive, noninvasive functional method for diagnosing renovascular hypertension.

fields.Text: Captopril renal scintigraphy for renovascular hypertension typically uses {{c1::Tc-99m MAG3}}.

fields.Text:
A fetus's occupational dose limit is what percentage of a nonpregnant radiation worker's annual dose limit?

{{c1::10%}}.

Q14. Correct; difficulty: moderate. It is important to commit to memory the various radiation dose limits, as they are frequently tested.

fields.Text: Tc-99m MDP/HDP bone scan radiopharmaceuticals localize by {{c1::adsorption to hydroxyapatite/crystals in bone matrix}}.
fields.Extra:
Q15. Correct; difficulty: easy. Tc99m-MDP, which is used for performing different types of bone scans, as shown in the image, acts by adsorption to the hydroxyapatite crystals in bone.





Open review Q15

fields.Text:
Hemochromatosis liver MRI. Liver signal compared with normal?

{{c1::Lower than normal}}
fields.Extra:
Q16. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Hemochromatosis is a condition caused by the chronic accumulation of iron due to a variety of causes, including hereditary hemochromatosis, repeated blood transfusions, excessive iron therapy, or liver disease. In hemochromatosis, the liver on in-phase sequence (which is usually obtained second, and thus more susceptible to T2* effects) demonstrates low signal, whereas the out-of-phase sequence demonstrates higher signal. On T2 weighted imaging (as in this case), the liver signal is lower than muscle in the setting of hemochromatosis.




Open review Q16

fields.Text: In hemochromatosis, hepatic iron causes the liver to show {{c1::low signal on T2/T2*-sensitive imaging}}.
fields.Extra:
Q16. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Hemochromatosis is a condition caused by the chronic accumulation of iron due to a variety of causes, including hereditary hemochromatosis, repeated blood transfusions, excessive iron therapy, or liver disease. In hemochromatosis, the liver on in-phase sequence (which is usually obtained second, and thus more susceptible to T2* effects) demonstrates low signal, whereas the out-of-phase sequence demonstrates higher signal. On T2 weighted imaging (as in this case), the liver signal is lower than muscle in the setting of hemochromatosis.

fields.Text:
Apical lung mass with facial plethora/supraclavicular swelling. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Pancoast tumor}}
fields.Extra:
Q17. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: A Pancoast tumor is a malignant tumor of the pulmonary apex that often is non-small cell carcinoma. It can classically present with Horner syndrome (ipsilateral ptosis, miosis, enophthalmos, and anhidrosis), shoulder pain, and/or signs and symptoms of neurovascular compression of the upper extremity.

fields.Text:
Breast mass with benign intramammary lymph node features. BI-RADS?

{{c1::BI-RADS 2}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q18. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Normal intramammary lymph nodes are circumscribed oval masses <1cm with hilar fat - abnormal features warranting biopsy include cortical thickening ≥3mm (cancer patients) or ≥5mm (screening patients), loss of hilar fat, or interval change, with metastatic nodes being poor prognostic indicators that alter TNM staging.

fields.Text: A normal intramammary lymph node is a circumscribed oval mass {{c1::<1 cm with hilar fat}} and is BI-RADS 2.

fields.Text: Among common breast cancer risk factors, {{c1::extremely dense breasts in a 36-year-old}} is lower risk than older age, BRCA mutation, atypia, or prior chest radiation.
fields.Extra:
Q19. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Risk factors for breast cancer are generally related to advancing age, family history (first-degree relatives, BRCA1/2 mutations), hormonal factors (early menarche, late menopause, nulliparity, late first pregnancy after age 30, prolonged hormone replacement therapy), race/ethnicity, personal history of breast cancer or atypical hyperplasia, dense breast tissue, prior chest radiation, obesity, and alcohol consumption.

fields.Text: Image Gently emphasizes pediatric dose optimization, not simply {{c1::obtaining the best image quality possible regardless of dose}}.
fields.Extra:
Q20. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Images need only be diagnostic quality, and do not need to be perfect, as that often leads to techniques with an unnecessarily high radiation dosage.

fields.Text: Image Gently is built around {{c1::pediatric radiation dose reduction/optimization}}, not maximum image quality at any dose.
fields.Extra:

fields.Text: A patient first learning about a known cancer diagnosis during an IR consultation is an example of {{c1::diagnostic error/communication failure}}.
fields.Extra:
Q21. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Explanation: A diagnostic error is, according to 2015 Institute of Medicine (IOM) report (page 12 of the American Board of Radiology (ABR) non-interpretive skills manual): "failure to (a) establish an accurate and timely explanation of the patient's health problem(s) or (b) communicate that explanation to the patient".

fields.Text:
AEC/grid geometry diagram. Which setup gives the highest patient dose?

{{c1::Grid plus smaller patient-to-source distance}}
fields.Extra:
Q23. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: With AEC, increasing patient distance from x-ray source decreases patient dose because the patient benefits from inverse square law protection even though AEC increases technique to compensate. Adding a grid increases patient dose as AEC compensates for grid absorption by raising exposure factors.

fields.Text: For Tc-99m studies cleared by the kidneys, the dose-limiting critical organ is often the {{c1::bladder}}.
fields.Extra:
Q24. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The clearance of the radiotracer from the soft tissues is by the kidneys and collects in bladder. Bladder toxicity is the limiting factor when performing a technetium-99m study.

Which commonly used generator parent isotope is typically fission-produced?

{{c1::Molybdenum-99}}.

fields.Text:
I-123 MIBG scan in child with abdominal mass. Diagnosis and uptake mechanism?

{{c1::Neuroblastoma; presynaptic adrenergic nerve uptake}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q31. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: I-123 MIBG demonstrates presynaptic uptake via the norepinephrine transporter on sympathetic nerve terminals. Neuroblastoma cells retain this presynaptic catecholamine transport mechanism from their neural crest origin, enabling specific tumor targeting with high specificity for staging and potential I-131 MIBG therapy.

fields.Text: I-123 MIBG targets neuroblastoma through {{c1::presynaptic norepinephrine transporter uptake on sympathetic nerve terminals}}.

MRCP-type heavily T2-weighted image. Expected TR/TE pattern?

{{c1::Long TR and long TE}}
fields.Extra:
Q36. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: MRCP techniques use heavily T2-weighted sequences with long echo times to depict the fluid within the biliary ductal system as high signal intensity, while the background signal intensity from liver and other parenchymal organs is suppressed.

fields.Text: A painful well-circumscribed medullary lesion with chondroid calcifications warrants {{c1::short-interval follow-up and consideration of advanced imaging}}.
fields.Extra:
Q37. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: A painful well-circumscribed medullary lesion with coarse calcifications in a young adult raises a differential including enchondroma, early low-grade chondrosarcoma, and intraosseous lipoma with dystrophic calcification. Pain alone warrants advanced imaging, as low-grade chondrosarcoma can be radiographically indistinguishable from enchondroma without MRI or CT correlation.

fields.Text:
Midline pelvic cystic lesion in adult male with calcification/dysuria. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Calcified prostatic utricle cyst}}
fields.Extra:
Q38. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Prostatic utricle cysts are small (<10 mm) pear-shaped T2 hyperintense midline cysts that rarely extend beyond the prostate superior border, distinguishing them from larger Mullerian duct cysts which characteristically extend cephalad beyond the prostate.

fields.Text: A prostatic utricle cyst is a small {{c1::midline pear-shaped cyst that usually does not extend above the prostate}}.

fields.Text: Left atrial myxoma causing exertional syncope is treated with {{c1::surgical resection}} to prevent obstruction and embolic complications.
fields.Extra:
Q39. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Intracardiac tumors are usually benign, and most are myxomas. The most common location is the left atrium. The mass can obstruct the mitral valve, causing impaired cardiac output and syncope. Surgical resection is necessary to avoid thrombi and stroke.

How quickly should an NRC-defined medical event be reported by telephone?

{{c1::By the next calendar day}}.
fields.Extra:
Q43. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The NRC specifically states “The licensee shall notify by telephone the NRC Operations Center no later than the next calendar day after discovery of the medical event” in 10 CFR Part 35 – Subpart M.




Open review Q43

fields.Text:
Young patient with LLQ pain and complex ovarian cyst. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Hemorrhagic cyst}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q44. Correct; difficulty: easy. The ultrasound of the left adnexa demonstrates a circumscribed mass with lacelike internal echoes and no flow on color Doppler imaging and through transmission posteriorly.




Open review Q44

fields.Text: A hemorrhagic ovarian cyst commonly has {{c1::reticular/lacy internal echoes from fibrin strands}}.
fields.Extra:
Q44. Correct; difficulty: easy. The ultrasound of the left adnexa demonstrates a circumscribed mass with lacelike internal echoes and no flow on color Doppler imaging and through transmission posteriorly.






Open review Q44

fields.Text:
Chest radiograph with Sprengel deformity/omovertebral bone. Associated syndrome?

{{c1::Klippel-Feil syndrome}}
fields.Extra:
Q45. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Klippel-Feil syndrome is a complex heterogeneous entity that results in cervical vertebral fusion. Klippel-Feil syndrome is associated with Sprengel's deformity of the shoulder, characterized by elevation of the scapulae with a bone bridge from the left scapula to the cervical spine (arrow) consistent with an omovertebral bone.





Open review Q45

fields.Text: Klippel-Feil syndrome results in {{c1::cervical vertebral fusion}} and is associated with Sprengel deformity/omovertebral bone.
fields.Extra:
Q45. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Klippel-Feil syndrome is a complex heterogeneous entity that results in cervical vertebral fusion. Klippel-Feil syndrome is associated with Sprengel's deformity of the shoulder, characterized by elevation of the scapulae with a bone bridge from the left scapula to the cervical spine (arrow) consistent with an omovertebral bone.






Open review Q45

fields.Text: A 2.5 cm solid thyroid nodule with macrocalcifications is at least {{c1::ACR TI-RADS TR3}} and meets FNA size threshold.
fields.Extra:
Q46. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A 2.5 cm solid nodule with macrocalcifications would be at least TR3 under ACR TI-RADS (3 points: 2 for solid composition, 1 for macrocalcifications) and meets the size threshold for fine-needle aspiration since TR3 nodules are biopsied at 2.5 cm.





Open review Q46

fields.Text:
Screening mammogram quality issue. Conclusion?

{{c1::Suboptimal breast positioning}}
fields.Extra:
Q47. Correct; difficulty: hard. Breast positioning is the key factor affecting a mammogram.




Open review Q47

fields.Text: A screening mammogram with inadequate tissue inclusion/compression should be recognized as {{c1::suboptimal breast positioning}}.
fields.Extra:
Q47. Correct; difficulty: hard. Breast positioning is the key factor affecting a mammogram.





Open review Q47

fields.Text:
Thoracic spine MRI with scalpel sign and syrinx. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Dorsal thoracic arachnoid web}}

1/5
fields.Extra:
Q48. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Dorsal arachnoid webs cause characteristic focal dorsal cord indentation (scalpel sign) with compensatory ventral cord displacement and associated syringomyelia due to altered CSF dynamics. The web itself is rarely directly visualized, making recognition of these secondary imaging signs crucial for diagnosis and appropriate surgical referral for web lysis.






Open review Q48

fields.Text: A dorsal thoracic arachnoid web causes the {{c1::scalpel sign}} from focal dorsal cord indentation and ventral cord displacement.
fields.Extra:
Q48. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Dorsal arachnoid webs cause characteristic focal dorsal cord indentation (scalpel sign) with compensatory ventral cord displacement and associated syringomyelia due to altered CSF dynamics. The web itself is rarely directly visualized, making recognition of these secondary imaging signs crucial for diagnosis and appropriate surgical referral for web lysis.











Open review Q48

fields.Text:
What is the most common primary malignancy causing cardiac metastasis?

{{c1::Lung cancer}}.
fields.Extra:
Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Lung cancer is the most common cause of cardiac metastasis, accounting for nearly 40% of cases, primarily driven by high prevalence in the general population and overall aggressive behavior.

fields.Text: Cardiac metastases most often arise from {{c1::lung cancer}}, mainly because of lung cancer's prevalence and aggressive behavior.

fields.Text:
Breast ultrasound with snowstorm sign after fall in a patient with implants. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Extracapsular silicone implant rupture}}
fields.Extra:
Q51. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Extracapsular silicone rupture on ultrasound shows the snowstorm sign - focal nodules with widespread increased echogenicity and loss of normal parenchymal boundaries as silicone escapes beyond the fibrous capsule.

fields.Text:
Athlete with acute foot pain and inability to bear weight. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Lisfranc injury}}
fields.Extra:
Q53. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Lisfranc injuries, also called Lisfranc fracture-dislocations, are the most common type of dislocation involving the foot and correspond to the dislocation of the articulation of the tarsus with the metatarsal bases.

fields.Text: Lisfranc fracture-dislocations generally require {{c1::surgical management}} when unstable/displaced.

fields.Text:
X-ray spectrum after added filtration. Key change?

{{c1::Reduced low-energy photons and lower overall intensity}}
fields.Extra:
Q54. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Filtration, in this case, results in decrease in the amount of photons on the lower end of the energy spectrum (movement of the lower energy x-intercept to the right) as well as a decrease in overall relative intensity (overall downward movement of the curve along the y-axis).




Open review Q54

fields.Text: Adding x-ray filtration {{c1::removes low-energy photons and reduces overall beam intensity}}.
fields.Extra:
Q54. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Filtration, in this case, results in decrease in the amount of photons on the lower end of the energy spectrum (movement of the lower energy x-intercept to the right) as well as a decrease in overall relative intensity (overall downward movement of the curve along the y-axis).





Open review Q54

fields.Text:
Who should perform nuchal translucency measurements?

{{c1::Facilities/sonographers with special certification}}.
fields.Extra:
Q55. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Nuchal translucency is the normal fluid-filled subcutaneous space identified at the back of the fetal neck during the late first trimester and early second trimester (11 weeks 3 days to 13 weeks 6 days). Increased nuchal translucency is associated with fetal abnormality, and increases are also directly proportional to the risk of miscarriage and fetal demise. Measurement should be made in facilities certified by the Fetal Medicine Foundation.




Open review Q55

fields.Text: Nuchal translucency is technically standardized and should be measured only by {{c1::certified facilities/sonographers}}.
